features.summary: Deere reported Q2 2025 results with net sales down 16% to $12.8B and equipment operations margin of 18.8%, exceeding expectations despite challenging market conditions. Production and Precision Ag sales fell 21% while Small Ag and Turf declined 6%. The company faced approximately $100M in tariff headwinds during the quarter but delivered strong operational performance through factory efficiency gains and cost management. Full-year guidance was maintained at the high end but ranges were widened due to tariff uncertainty, with net income projected between $4.75-5.5B.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued market volatility through the remainder of fiscal 2025, with large Ag equipment industry sales in the U.S. and Canada projected down approximately 30%. The company anticipates pre-tax tariff impacts of just over $500M for the full year if current levels persist. Despite near-term headwinds, management remains committed to its smart industrial strategy and announced plans to invest $20B in U.S. operations over the next decade. Early signs of sentiment improvement are emerging in South America and Europe, though North American markets remain challenged by trade uncertainty and elevated used equipment inventory.
features.risks: Primary risks include ongoing trade policy volatility with tariffs creating $500M+ annual cost pressure, elevated late-model used equipment inventory in North America weighing on new equipment demand, and high interest rates pressuring customer purchase decisions. The company faces significant tariff exposure through component sourcing from Mexico, Europe, and China, with Construction & Forestry bearing disproportionate impact due to supply agreements and overseas production. Market uncertainty continues to dampen customer sentiment across all segments, while pricing power remains constrained given recent years of significant price increases.
features.key_drivers: Technology adoption continues accelerating with Precision Essentials orders reaching nearly 10,000 globally in the first half, exceeding all of fiscal 2024. See & Spray utilization is expanding with over 1,000 new orders and existing customers showing increased usage and additional unit purchases. The company is progressing toward autonomous tillage commercialization in model year 2026 and expanding its technology platform across business units. Operational excellence initiatives are delivering material cost savings and factory efficiency gains, while the company maintains strong dealer relationships and customer loyalty during the downturn.
